book_claims.claim: Whole-grain cracker used as a side in the Longevity Diet.

supplementary_research.finding: Rye consumption significantly improves insulin sensitivity and reduces postprandial insulin response compared to wheat; rye's unique fiber composition is responsible.
Whole-grain rye diet reduces inflammatory markers (CRP, IL-6) and improves gut microbiota composition compared to refined wheat in a 12-week intervention.

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Among the highest prebiotic fiber of any grain; selectively feeds Bifidobacterium and Lactobacillus
Soluble fiber forming viscous gel; reduces cholesterol absorption and slows glucose release
Unique rye phenolics; antioxidant and anti-carcinogenic in cell studies

synergies.description: Omega-3s in salmon combined with rye fiber create potent anti-inflammatory and cardiovascular-protective combination
EPA/DHA from sardines and arabinoxylan from rye together reduce triglycerides and support gut microbiome diversity
Olive oil's oleocanthal anti-inflammatory effects complement rye's alkylresorcinols for combined NF-kB inhibition
